title: Eurycaulis treubii
taxonomy: Eurycaulis treubii is classified in the family Santalaceae, which is a family of parasitic plants commonly known as mistletoes. This family is part of the order Santalales, which includes about 1,000 to 1,900 species of flowering plants. The Santalales order is a group of flowering plants that are generally considered to be parasitic or hemiparasitic, meaning they obtain some of their nutrients from other plants. Eurycaulis treubii is a species of mistletoe that is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. It is a hemiparasitic plant that obtains some of its nutrients from the trees on which it grows.
